svg.title: Detailed OS-level recording architecture with iCloud sync chain
svg.desc: Full data flow from audio and video sources through OBS, into a Python watcher pipeline (FFmpeg, Whisper.cpp, Ollama), and through macOS iCloud sync daemons to other Apple devices. Highlights the trigger mechanism (FSEvents) and the automatic macOS sync chain.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Google Meet emit client audio?')
sendPrompt('How does the Snowball wire into OBS?')
sendPrompt('How does the Multi-Output Device split audio?')
sendPrompt('Why use BlackHole instead of native macOS audio capture?')
sendPrompt('Walk me through the OBS audio track settings again.')
g.text: Track 1 mic · Track 2 system audio · Video screen and webcam
Triggered by macOS FSEvents on new .mp4

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does FFmpeg do in the pipeline?')
sendPrompt('How does Whisper.cpp produce the transcript?')

svg.text: macOS iCloud Sync · automatic, handled by the OS

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Path.replace() do to move files to iCloud?')
sendPrompt('What are bird and cloudd, and what do they do?')
sendPrompt('How do my iPhone and iPad receive the synced files?')
